statement: Nothing may be presented as the user's language that does not come from him — an item attributed to him that he does not recognize as his own words, and cannot even parse the meaning of, is invalid
recurrence: single two-message exchange 2026-07-19T00:03-00:04Z rejecting an audit item (DA-01) as not his language and unintelligible; sharpens IN-125/IN-126 (exact data, never invented) into an attribution rule for items anchored on the user

history.note: new-window sweep of session c41e910c, 2026-07-17 to 2026-07-19

statement: Canonical policy text must state the policy exactly as the user set it, aligned to his citable intentions in the record; hedged residue contradicting the set policy — an iteration cap the iterate-to-zero policy does not have — is shoddy LLM hedging and an untruth requiring immediate rectification
recurrence: pressed across the 2026-07-18T17:01-17:16Z exchange (why does CLAUDE.md still say cap; why is the word cap even mentioned); the underlying no-cap termination rule is IN-131 recurring — the new intent is the doc-truth requirement that canonical text carry no hedged residue against the citable record

history.note: new-window sweep of session c41e910c, 2026-07-17 to 2026-07-19

statement: Canonical policy prose is itself subject to the rhetorical register policies — governing text must be clear and easy to follow; jargon-dense prose that no one could claim with a straight face to be clear is a violation regardless of accuracy
recurrence: twice in the 2026-07-18T17:09-17:17Z exchange, once flagging proposed prose as a rhetorical-register violation and once defying anyone to call the committed mandate-008 paragraph clear; extends IN-61 (plain English, no opaque harness jargon in code comments) to canonical policy documents
